payload.text:
Il faut que le calendly se charge beaucoup plus vite car la c'est trop lent, et refait le design de la section avis avec comme si que c'était des vrais avis google. Il faut qu'on puisse les defilé à l'horizontal : voici un vrai avis donc fais dans ce genre : Melissa est une superbe thérapeute de couple, que j'ai consulté sur les conseils d'une amie, très satisfaite de son approche.
Melissa est très à l'écoute bienveillante et surtout ne porte aucun jugement sur votre problématique. Elle inspire la confiance et
rassure. Grâce à elle nous avons réussi à sauver notre couple à un moment où je pensais que plus rien n'était possible. Merci du fond du cœur à Melissa pour tout ce qu'elle à fait pour mon mari et moi. Je la recommande vivement et surtout gardez en tête que rien n'est jamais perdu avec Melissa.

Thérapeute à l’écoute et professionnel. Grâce à la thérapie, nous avons retrouvé une meilleure communication et apaisé nos tensions. Recommandé pour tous les couples en quête de solutions concrètes.

Nous avons rencontré Melissa pour une thérapie de couple .On est arrivé chez elle démunie notre couple étais au point mort plus de communication verbale ,on parler que par message pour nos enfants.le couple étais oublié. On étais ensemble que pour nos enfants.
Mélissa nous a aidé à réapprendre à dialogué ensemble et nous faire comprendre que les mots on plusieurs signification.
Aujourd’hui nous avons les bases pour que notre couple continue sur les bases solide qu’on avez perdu.
Merci à vous Mélissa vous avez sauvez mon couple à la séparation .
C’est pas facile de se dévoiler quand on connaît pas une personne, il faut savoir que Melissa est là pour nous écouter et non pour nous juger. tout ce qui est dit dans son bureau, reste dans son bureau, elle est pas là pour nous rabaisser, mais pour nous donner des solutions.
Maintenant ses à nous de continuer les efforts et de pas oublier il les important de ceux parler .

**Pinpointing the Problem**

I'm currently focused on the performance of the Calendly widget. It's noticeably slow, so I'm digging into potential causes. I also want to make the reviews section design feel more authentic, aiming for a visual style that mirrors Google Reviews.


**Optimizing Calendly Loading**

I've been evaluating the Calendly widget's sluggishness. The current lazy-loading setup using `IntersectionObserver`, while good for initial load times, isn't cutting it. I'm considering preconnecting to `assets.calendly.com`, or just loading the script earlier. Perhaps I'll load it immediately with `async` in the `<head>` or at the end of `<body>` to resolve this performance problem.


**Adjusting Calendly Integration**

I've discarded the intersection observer approach for Calendly. The optimal solution is now clear: an `async` script tag loaded at the end of the `<body>`.  `loading="eager"` isn't applicable to the widget's script, but I'm confident this placement will significantly improve perceived performance.


**Crafting Review Carousel**

I'm now shifting gears to the review section's visual design. It's time to create the carousel. I've decided on Open Sans for the text.  The core layout will be a horizontal scroll. Now, I'm defining the content structure and styling to make it look like Google Reviews.  This includes a white background, a distinct shadow, and relative date text. I will use the `.no-scrollbar` class for hiding the scrollbar on the carousel.


**Fine-tuning the Carousel**

I've been working on the structure and look of the review carousel. I've got the overall HTML container with the scroll behavior down. Now, I'm focusing on refining each card element to match the Google Reviews aesthetic. I'm focusing on the header of the card, the structure for the stars (I added an X months ago, using a relative date), and the actual text content. I've also incorporated a menu icon.


**Constructing Review Cards**

I'm now diving into the code for the review carousel cards. I'm focusing on crafting the structure of each card element. I'm adding gold star SVG icons and integrating the relative date text "il y a X semaines/mois".  I'm debating how to handle long text: whether to show it fully, clamp it, or include a "Lire plus" link. Given it's a horizontal scroll, full visibility might make the cards uneven, so I'll either make them fixed height or min-height, or simply let them expand and align at the top for consistency.


**Completing Review Details**

I'm finalizing the content of the review carousel cards. For the reviews, I'm integrating reviewer names like "Madame R." or "Marc D." to add authenticity. This is in accordance with my earlier decision to use appropriate names.  Now I'm making sure each card captures all the review details accurately, to create a final, authentic look. I am adding a 4.9 or 5.0 star rating summary.


**Designing the Review Cards**

I'm now in the process of finalizing the look and feel of the individual review cards within the carousel. I've decided to use Roboto or Arial for the text, and orange or yellow for the star ratings. I'm focusing on the layout to reflect a Google Reviews aesthetic: an avatar on the left and the reviewer's name at the top. I'll also ensure horizontal scrolling is enabled with `overflow-x-auto`.

To address your request, I will make two major changes:

1.  **Calendly Performance:** I will remove the "lazy loading" script (which waits for the user to scroll to the section before loading the widget) and instead load the Calendly script immediately with the `async` attribute. This ensures the calendar is ready and rendered instanty when the user arrives at the section.
2.  **Reviews Redesign:** I will completely rebuild the `#avis` section to look exactly like the **Google Maps Review cards**.
    *   I will use the specific text you provided.
    *   I will implement a horizontal scrolling list (Carousel).
    *   I will style the cards with the authentic Google avatar, stars, and layout.

Here is the updated code:

entries.description: Optimize Calendly loading speed and redesign Reviews section to look like authentic Google Reviews with horizontal scroll

payload.text:
**Assessing Mobile Interaction**

I'm currently thinking about improving the mobile user experience. The "swipe to see more reviews" text feels a bit clunky. My new idea involves using small dots, like pagination indicators, instead. This might feel more intuitive. The change should be small and feel natural.


**Implement Calendar Integration**

I've shifted focus to integrating a scheduling widget, specifically `Cal.com`, into the site.  My immediate task is updating `index.html` to accommodate the embed code.  Next, I'll work on customizing the widget's appearance to match the site's color scheme for a seamless user experience.


**Visualize Carousel Pagination**

I'm now focused on the pagination of the carousel. Ditching the text entirely, I'm leaning towards a simple row of four dots at the base, with the active one highlighted to guide the user. CSS will be key to implementing this static visual indicator to suggest that the content is a slider.  The "..." seems a bit too simple, and standard pagination is probably best.


**Customize Cal.com Styling**

I'm now diving into the styling of the embedded Cal.com widget. My focus is ensuring a seamless integration with the website's color scheme. I'm exploring the `theme` and `styles` parameters offered by Cal.com to achieve a light, Sage-Cream aesthetic, which will align with the existing site design.
